CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, value INTEGER, status VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, NULL, 'active'), (2, 10, 'inactive'), (3, NULL, 'active');
UPDATE test_table SET value = 0 WHERE value IS NULL;
SELECT * FROM test_table;
UPDATE test_table SET value = 1, status = 'updated' WHERE status = 'active';
CREATE TABLE original_table AS SELECT 1 AS key, 'old_value' AS value UNION ALL SELECT 2 AS key, 'old_value_2' AS value;
CREATE TABLE new_table AS SELECT 1 AS key, 'new_value' AS value UNION ALL SELECT 2 AS key, 'new_value_2' AS value;
UPDATE original_table SET value = new_table.value FROM new_table WHERE original_table.key = new_table.key;
UPDATE original_table SET value = (SELECT new_table.value FROM new_table WHERE original_table.key = new_table.key);
SELECT * FROM original_table;
UPDATE original_table AS base_table SET value = (SELECT new_table.value || ' updated' FROM new_table WHERE base_table.key = new_table.key);
CREATE TABLE city_table (name VARCHAR, revenue BIGINT, country_code VARCHAR);
CREATE TABLE country_table (code VARCHAR, name VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO city_table VALUES ('Paris', 700, 'FR'), ('Lyon', 200, 'FR'), ('Brussels', 400, 'BE');
INSERT INTO country_table VALUES ('FR', 'France'), ('BE', 'Belgium');
UPDATE city_table SET revenue = revenue + 100 FROM country_table WHERE city_table.country_code = country_table.code AND country_table.name = 'France';
SELECT * FROM city_table;
CREATE TABLE sales (id INTEGER, amount BIGINT, region VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO sales VALUES (1, 1000, 'North'), (2, 1500, 'South'), (3, 2000, 'East');
UPDATE sales SET amount = amount * 1.1 WHERE region = 'North';
UPDATE sales SET amount = (SELECT SUM(amount) FROM sales WHERE region != 'South') WHERE region = 'South';
UPDATE sales SET amount = 0 WHERE amount IS NULL;
